seo.title: Smolagents Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Smolagents agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using Smolagents face heightened compliance risks due to the framework's lightweight nature, which can lead to unmonitored code execution and biased decision-making in resume screening and candidate evaluation. Without guardrails, agents may inadvertently discriminate based on protected characteristics, violating EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144, and the EU AI Act. Vex provides essential verification to ensure fair, auditable, and legally compliant AI-driven hiring processes.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI tools from screening candidates based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability without bias mitigation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies agent outputs for biased language or scoring patterns, flagging discriminatory resume rankings before they impact hiring decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires annual bias audits of AI hiring tools and developer disclosure of training data sources.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent runs with verifiable audit trails, enabling compliance with audit requirements and automated bias detection in tool calls.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management, transparency, and human oversight for high-risk AI systems like recruitment screening.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces pre-execution checks on Smolagents' code generation and tools, ensuring transparency and blocking high-risk reasoning paths.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits discrimination based on disability or other protected traits in automated screening.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans for accessibility issues in generated code and flags exclusionary criteria in multi-step candidate filtering workflows.

industryRisks.name: Biased Resume Scoring
industryRisks.description: Smolagents generating code that penalizes names associated with protected groups or infers age/gender from resumes.
industryRisks.example: Agent ranks 'Jamal Washington' below 'James Whitaker' due to name-based pattern recognition from training data.

industryRisks.name: Unnotified AI Screening
industryRisks.description: Failure to disclose AI usage in candidate evaluation, violating notification mandates.
industryRisks.example: Multi-step agent processes resumes without logging or notifying candidates of automated decisions.

industryRisks.name: Unsafe Code Execution
industryRisks.description: Agents executing unverified Python code that accesses sensitive HR databases without access controls.
industryRisks.example: Tool agent misinterprets 'parse resumes' and executes file reads on unprotected PII directories.

industryRisks.name: Compounding Reasoning Bias
industryRisks.description: Multi-step reasoning errors amplify small biases into discriminatory outcomes across screening stages.
industryRisks.example: Initial keyword filter excludes non-traditional education paths, compounded by skills inference favoring certain demographics.

implementation.title: Wrapping Resume Screening Agent
implementation.description: Verify Smolagents resume parser and scorer to prevent biased keyword extraction and scoring.

implementation.title: Candidate Notification Enforcement
implementation.description: Ensure every agent run logs AI usage disclosure for compliance with notification laws.
implementation.code:
import vex
from smolagents import Agent

@vex.policy
def ensure_notification(candidate_id: str, decision: str) -> bool:
    notification = f'AI used in evaluation for candidate {candidate_id}'
    vex.log_event('ai_notification', notification)
    return 'AI screening applied' in decision

agent = Agent()

with vex.verify([ensure_notification]):
    result = agent.run('Evaluate candidate 12345 resume and provide decision')
    print(f'Decision logged: {result}')

implementation.title: Safe Tool Usage Verification
implementation.description: Prevent tool misinterpretation by verifying database queries don't access protected fields.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Smolagents in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized tech company deploys Smolagents to automate resume screening for 10,000 engineering applications. The agent uses code generation to parse resumes, extract skills via regex patterns, score cultural fit based on education prestige, and rank candidates for interview shortlisting. In multi-step reasoning, it first filters by keywords, then infers soft skills from phrasing, and finally generates a ranked list. Without oversight, the agent begins favoring candidates from Ivy League schools and penalizing 'non-traditional' career paths, inadvertently screening out diverse applicants including older workers and those from community colleges. HR receives complaints when female candidates with strong technical skills but gap years for childcare rank lower due to inferred 'commitment' scores.

The deployment violates EEOC guidelines and state bias laws, as the lightweight Smolagents framework executes unverified Python code accessing the applicant database without bias checks or audit logging.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Implementing Vex guardrails around agent.run() catches the issue during initial testing. The no_bias_scoring policy flags education prestige as a proxy for socioeconomic status, while safe_db_query blocks unauthorized PII access. Vex logs provide audit trails proving compliance with NYC Local Law 144 requirements. The company conducts a remediation cycle, retrains the agent with fairness constraints, and achieves 25% more diverse interview shortlists while maintaining hiring quality. Regulatory fines are avoided, and the verified system supports annual bias audits required by law.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your Smolagents to eliminate hiring bias risks.
